steps.title: Isolate the term containing k
steps.content: Start with \(14j + 5k = m\). Subtract \(14j\) from both sides: \(5k = m - 14j\).

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 5: \(k = \dfrac{m - 14j}{5}\).
  • A (\(k = \frac{m - 14j}{5}\)): Matches exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B (\(k = \frac{1}{5}m - 14j\)): Only \(m\) is divided by 5; \(14j\) should also be divided by 5 — wrong.
  • C (\(k = \frac{14j - m}{5}\)): Numerator is negated; subtracting \(14j\) gives \(m - 14j\), not \(14j - m\) — wrong sign.
  • D (\(k = 5m - 14j\)): Multiplies \(m\) by 5 instead of dividing — wrong operation.
